      h.map_prod hX.aemeasurable hY.aemeasurable, hX.map_eq, hY.map_eq, bernoulliOn_eq_map,
      bernoulliOn_eq_map, bernoulliOn_eq_map, ← map_map, ← map_map, map_map,
      ← Measure.map_map _ (MeasurableEquiv.piProdPi (fun _ ↦ Bool) (fun _ ↦ Bool)).measurable,
      Measure.pi_prod]
    simp_rw [map_map, Measure.map_pi, Function.comp, MeasurableEquiv.piProdPi_apply]
    congr with i
    simp only [toNNReal, ENNReal.smul_def, map_add, map_smul, map_dirac]
    by_cases hi : i ∈ u <;> simp [hi, mul_assoc]
    · rw [← smul_add, ← smul_comm (a := (p : ENNReal)), smul_smul, ← ENNReal.coe_mul]
      congr; ext x; cases x <;> simp
    · simp

by
    rw [← IndepFun.map_prod hXY hX.aemeasurable hY.aemeasurable, hX.map_eq, hY.map_eq,
      bernoulliOn_eq_map, bernoulliOn_eq_map, bernoulliOn_eq_map]
    simp_rw [Measure.map_map MeasurableEquiv.setOf.measurable (Measurable.prod_map MeasurableEquiv.setOf.measurable MeasurableEquiv.setOf.measurable)]
    congr 1
    -- The proof that the intersection of independent Bernoulli processes is a Bernoulli process
    -- relies on the fact that the product of Pi measures maps to a Pi measure under componentwise operation.
    sorry
